source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 5. Detailed Project Scope > 5.1 Task and Effort Estimates
text_content: Effort estimates for main activities will be updated as work proceeds and requirements are clarified. Estimates are by site and in workdays over the life of the project. The following table summarizes estimates on all activities to author and publish UA. Activities comprise: Feature or Scope items – Authoring and updating content for specific program features. General content – Maintaining UA (updates for corrections or changes from the prior release) and updating overall content not specific to features. CRNs – Formatting, finalizing content for publication, and publishing. Quality assurance and production – Reviews, including formal and technical (DITA XML QA) reviews, and revisions per review findings. Management and localization – Administration, meetings, coordination with GPL Localization on translation work, distribution.

source: D68685 HAL 4.0 Cybersecurity Statement Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. Acceptance procedures are utilized to provide a further barrier to loss of integrity, as the

Purpose and Scope of the UAP The UAP identifies deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective revised documentation for users of the Halcyon 2.0 system. This UAP serves as a communication vehicle for the Halcyon 2.0 project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. Writers are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up-to-date throughout the project and modified as necessary. If the key milestones remain unchanged, then details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

title: 2. Build Versioning
text_content: The system build versioning follows the product versioning instructions L6954 – Product Versioning. This versioning scheme is primarily used for formal builds generated for test readiness review. XX . YY . Zz BB • XX = Two numbers indicating major release • YY = Two numbers indicating minor release • Zz  = Two numbers indicating maintenance release • BB = Two numbers indicating engineering build number where o Left side ‘B’ is used for TRR number o Right side ‘B’ is used for build iteration within a given TRR number. The system build will have one or more component builds. These builds use an internal engineering build versioning scheme that differs from system build versioning.

Post market reviews are conducted annually or whenever new risks are identified or whenever there is a design change or impact to current risks. The Review Report must be signed by appropriate RACI approves of the risk management file. Varian processes for the gathering and analysis of production and post-production information are documented in the Medical Device Risk Management work instruction. text_content: Main control of content for accuracy, completeness, and quality is through reviews, internal and formal; UAD keystroking and tryout of new or changed features and procedures. Internal reviews are uncontrolled. They are intended to: Capture most changes before the formal review with longer review periods than the formal review. Ensure that all project team members have a chance to respond to the draft UA so that GPL can deliver the UA as planned. Resolve and reduce as many findings as possible before formal review. Formal reviews are controlled—findings are recorded—and require participation of the project team per RACI for Design and Development and GPL Authoring Process. For general design quality, see D95424 AL 4.0 MR2 Evolve Maintenance Release Document.

Requested Changes These changes will be included in the CRN:  The instructions for shutting down and restarting the system need to be revised based on the new hardware.  The CRN will be ported to the CMS to facilitate rebranding and to reduce localization costs. 
